items.title: Baseline binary tabular classifier
items.description: Train a perceptron (or sklearn Perceptron) on scaled numeric features as a fast linear baseline before trying SVM, logistic regression, or MLPs. Useful when classes are roughly separable and you need a simple interpretable boundary.

items.title: Hardware logic and early AI demos
items.description: Rosenblatt's Mark I Perceptron and textbook AND/OR gates show how a single unit implements linearly separable Boolean functions — historical and pedagogical foundation for neural networks.

items.title: Online learning from streaming labels
items.description: The mistake-driven update rule processes one example at a time without storing the full dataset — suitable for streaming spam flags or sensor thresholds when separability holds.

items.title: Feature sanity check before deep models
items.description: If a single perceptron cannot reach reasonable training accuracy on engineered features, the problem may not be linearly separable in that space — signal to add features, kernels, hidden layers, or nonlinear models.
